full_text:
Trending
Kochi: After a break of nearly two weeks, light to moderate south westerly clouds have formed over Arabian Sea. India meteorological department (IMD) issued yellow warning of heavy rainfall for various districts till June 25.Alappuzha, Ernakulam, Thrissur, Malappuram, Kozhikode, Wayanad, Kannur and Kasaragod will be on yellow warning on Monday. Strong winds with speed up to 60kmph is likely. IMD warned that thunderstorm accompanied by lightning was very likely to occur at one or two places over Kerala on Monday. It issued strong surface wind warning in the state till Tuesday.Meanwhile, Kerala is facing 28% deficiency in rainfall that began on June 1 till Sunday morning. Only Kozhikode, Malappuram, Pathanamthitta and Thiruvananthapuram have received normal rainfall so far. The remaining districts are facing rainfall deficiency. The highest deficiency is reported in Wayanad and Idukki at 56% and 50%, respectively.
